scanServices.name: Ultrasound / Sonography
scanServices.preparation: Full bladder required for pelvic/abdomen scans – drink 4-6 glasses of water 1 hour before. No preparation needed for other areas.

scanServices.name: 2D Echocardiography (2D Echo)
scanServices.preparation: No special preparation required. Avoid heavy meals 2 hours before.
scanServices.notes: Cardiologist report available. Please bring previous ECG/Echo reports if any.

scanServices.name: Upper GI Endoscopy (OGD Scope)
scanServices.preparation: STRICTLY FASTING for minimum 8 hours before procedure. No food, liquids, or water. Arrange for someone to accompany you as sedation may be used.
scanServices.reportingTime: Immediate verbal report. Written report same day.
scanServices.notes: Performed by Dr. Suresh Rao (Gastroenterologist). Mild sedation available on request.

scanServices.name: Colonoscopy
scanServices.preparation: Bowel preparation required: clear liquid diet 1 day before + laxative medication provided by doctor. Must fast from midnight before procedure.
scanServices.reportingTime: Immediate verbal report. Written report within 24 hours.
scanServices.notes: Performed by Dr. Suresh Rao. Escort required. Not suitable for patients with certain cardiac conditions – inform doctor.

scanServices.name: CBCT (Cone Beam CT Scan)
scanServices.preparation: Remove all metal objects (jewellery, spectacles, piercings). No other preparation required.
scanServices.notes: Primarily used for dental and maxillofacial imaging. Commonly used before dental implants or surgeries.

scanServices.name: OPG (Orthopantomogram / Dental X-Ray)
scanServices.preparation: Remove all dental appliances, jewellery. No other preparation needed.
scanServices.notes: Provides a panoramic view of the entire jaw, teeth, and surrounding structures.

scanServices.name: X-Ray
scanServices.preparation: No preparation required. Remove metal objects from the area being X-rayed. Inform technician if pregnant.
scanServices.notes: Various views available: Chest, Spine, Abdomen, Limbs etc. Doctor's referral may be needed for insurance claims.
